uk: Неділя 19-та по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 2. Єв. 8.
saints.en: 18th Sunday after Pentecost. Hieromartyr Hierotheus, Bishop of Athens. Ven. Francis of Assisi

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» – 10 стихир: 4 воскрес., 3 свщм., 3 прп., Слава: свщм, І нині: богородичний-догмат 2-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня. На стиховні: недільні стихири октоїха. Тропарі воскресний та свщмч. Слава: прп., І нині: богород. недільний за гласом тропаря прп. Відпуст великий з помин. недільним свщмч та прп.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар воскресний і свщмч., Слава: прп., І нині: богород. з недільних за гласом тропаря прп. Катизми чергові з воскресними сідальними, також Полієлей і «Ангельський собор», мала єктенія, іпакой, степенна і прокімен 2-го гласу. Єв. – Ів. 64 зач.; 20, 11-18. Все інше до канону – неділі. Канон воскресний, Богородиці, свщм. та прп. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». По 3-ій пісні – кондак-ікос свщмч. та прп. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос воскресні. По 9-ій – світильні недільний та свщмч., Слава: світильний прп., І нині: богород. світильного прп. Хвалитних стихир – 8: недільних октоїха чергового гласу. Слава: стихира євангельска, І нині: «Преблагословенна». По великому славослов'ї – тропар недільний «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий з помин. служби недільної, свщмч. та прп.
Часи: на всіх часах: тропар воскресний, Слава: на 3-му - свщмч., на 6-му - храму: на 9-му – прп. Конадаки: на 1-му – воскрес., на 3-му – свщмч, на 6-му - храму і на 9-му – прп.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Сл.: кондак воскрес., І нині: богород. з неділ. за гласом Сл. Прокімен і алилуя воскресні. Ап. – 2 Кор. 194 зач.; 11, 31 – 12, 9. Єв. – Лк. 26 зач.; 6, 31-36. Причасний «Хваліте».

troparion_en.text: We the faithful all praise Hierótheus, the disciple of Paul, / Hierarch of Athens, / the world’s teacher and a preacher of the Faith, / who revealed to us Christ’s Mysteries / and poured forth streams of godly doctrine. / His life was well-pleasing to God, who is greatly merciful.

kontakion_en.text: Hierarch of Athens, we praise you for you have instructed us in awesome and ineffable things, / and you were revealed to be a divinely-inspired writer of hymns. / Pray that we be delivered from every kind of sin, so that we may cry to you: / “Rejoice, divinely-wise Father Hierótheus.”

troparion.text: We the faithful all praise Hierótheus, the disciple of Paul, / Hierarch of Athens, / the world’s teacher and a preacher of the Faith, / who revealed to us Christ’s Mysteries / and poured forth streams of godly doctrine. / His life was well-pleasing to God, who is greatly merciful.

kontakion.text: Hierarch of Athens, we praise you for you have instructed us in awesome and ineffable things, / and you were revealed to be a divinely-inspired writer of hymns. / Pray that we be delivered from every kind of sin, so that we may cry to you: / “Rejoice, divinely-wise Father Hierótheus.”

oca_saints.name: Uncovering of the relics of Saint Gurias, first Archbishop of Kazan, and Saint Barsanuphius, Bishop of Tver

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Gurias, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Helladius of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Helladius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Onesimus of the Kiev Near Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Onesimus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Ammon the recluse of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Ammon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Gaius, Faustus, Eusebius, and Chaeremon, of Alexandria

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Peter, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Peter;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Domnina with her daughters of Syria

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Ammon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Paul the Simple of Egypt

troparion.text: In you, o venerable Father Paul, / the image of God shone forth, / for you carried your Cross and followed Christ. / By so doing you taught us to disregard the flesh for it passes away, / and to care instead for the soul since it is immortal, / wherefore your soul forever rejoices with the angels!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Evdemoz, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

In you the image was preserved with exactness, O Mother,  / for taking up your cross, you followed after Christ.  / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away,  / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal.  / Therefore, most venerable Mother Elena your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Having received the grace...“)
Because of your love of God you forsook a bed for rest, O Mother Elena, /  you illumined your soul
with fasting, thoroughly vanquishing your enemies; /  but by your intercessions destroy the evil designs of our adversaries.

oca_saints.name: Saints Jonah and Nectarius of Kazan

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy fathers Jonah and Nectarius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
